title: Telegram context loss: conversation_switch save/restore wipes in-session memory every turn
source: Ash live report 2026-06-22 â€” Pi has no memory of what was said 3 messages ago mid-conversation. Confirmed root cause.
current_state: _process_as_telegram_peer wraps every turn in conversation_switch(agent, 'telegram:{chat_id}'). conversation_switch saves agent.messages, loads from SQLite, processes the turn, then RESTORES the old agent.messages on exit. Persistence between turns relies on async SQLite writes via _async_log(mem.persist_turn, ...) completing before the next turn reloads from SQLite. Two failure modes: (1) messages arrive faster than the async queue flushes â†’ SQLite doesn't have the latest turns â†’ next turn starts with stale/partial history. (2) After /clear: agent.conversation_id stays 'telegram:{chat_id}' so conversation_switch sees target==current and skips the SQLite reload entirely â€” saved_messages=[] is restored every turn, making context permanently zero after /clear.
target_state: TelegramTools maintains a per-conv_id in-memory message cache (self._conv_cache: Dict[str, List]). _process_as_telegram_peer loads from SQLite exactly once on first contact per conv_id, then reads/writes the in-memory cache directly. After process_input, the updated agent.messages is saved back to the cache before restoring REPL state. The async SQLite writes (persist_turn) continue unchanged as crash-recovery insurance. /clear resets self._conv_cache[conv_id] = [] so the next turn starts fresh without reloading old SQLite turns. Photos, text, and button callbacks all go through _process_as_telegram_peer and thus share the same in-memory cache.
migration_plan: 1. TelegramTools.__init__: add self._conv_cache: Dict[str, List] = {} (keyed by conv_id string, e.g. 'telegram:123456').

2. Replace _process_as_telegram_peer entirely â€” remove conversation_switch import. New logic: (a) look up conv_id in _conv_cache; if missing, load from memory.load_conversation_turns(conv_id, max_turns=40) with [] fallback; (b) save/restore agent.conversation_id and agent.messages around the turn; (c) set agent.messages = _conv_cache[conv_id]; (d) call process_input; (e) _conv_cache[conv_id] = list(agent.messages) BEFORE restoring REPL state.

3. handle_clear (in _register_handlers): after clearing agent.messages, also do self._conv_cache[f'telegram:{chat_id}'] = [] so the cache is reset and next message starts fresh (no SQLite reload of old turns).

4. Tests: test_telegram_context_persistence.py â€” (a) two sequential calls to _process_as_telegram_peer use the same in-memory history (second call sees first turn); (b) /clear empties the cache; (c) first message after clear starts with empty context even if SQLite has old turns; (d) restart simulation (new TelegramTools instance) reloads from SQLite on first message.
risk_notes: conversation_switch was designed for autonomous background turns that need to temporarily borrow the agent and restore it cleanly. Removing it from the PRIMARY Telegram path is correct â€” it was never the right pattern for a persistent session. The save/restore of REPL state still happens (so root-mode REPL context isn't corrupted by Telegram turns), just without the SQLite-load-on-every-turn behavior.
